conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have a son named Alex who is 17 and lives with you at home.

Since Alex is over 16, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he attending college, sixth form, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, that meets one of the key requirements.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

Just a few more questions to complete the assessment: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, his other parent or another family member?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for extended periods, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of that. He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- You have a son named Alex who is 17 years old
- Alex is under 20 and is in approved education (doing A-levels at college)
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex lives at home with you full-time
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

According to the UK government rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex meets all these criteria, you are eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels)
- You live in the UK and are responsible for him
- He lives at home with you
- Nobody else is claiming for him

Please note: If either you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit even if the tax charge applies.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and under 20. He is in approved education (doing A-levels at college), which means the claimant still qualifies for Child Benefit even though Alex is over 16. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for Alex, who lives at home with them full-time. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels), the claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex who lives at home with them, and no one else is claiming for Alex. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
